# ++CHRCH Access

- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/controller-with-preconnect-5923ab14c9a2faa0ad2f0f95c16ea979.html#__shared_params__[version]=v3&__shared_params__[light_experiment_assignments]=%7B%22token%22%3A%22ac1381f1-85f9-408c-a0ed-8365965da108%22%2C%22assignments%22%3A%7B%22link_ewcs_prewarm_experiment_v2%22%3A%22treatment%22%7D%7D&apiKey=pk_live_hkgvgSBxG4TAl3zGlXiB1KUX&stripeJsId=ac1381f1-85f9-408c-a0ed-8365965da108&stripeObjId=sobj-3f0bca6f-ee15-4da2-ad99-66d181692281&firstStripeInstanceCreatedLatency=1167&controllerCount=1&isCheckout=false&stripeJsLoadTime=1778073373681&manualBrowserDeprecationRollout=false&mids[guid]=NA&mids[muid]=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&mids[sid]=0166e32d-cc6c-4a1c-b85e-97ed6eff4d1c0dcfe4&referrer=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F23846521&controllerId=__privateStripeController4051

---

##    7\. The Drill Down Method: How To Do It With A Partner That Needs To Do It Too 

Ooh, you're finally taking the leap!

Get ready for a fun ride...

### **Here are my suggestions:**

The key to doing the Drill Down with a partner is to leave a few days in between for some amnesia to settle in. This will keep it fresh for when you come across the same questions again with the other person.

The whole process should feel as intense as therapy. The whole focus should be on going down the rabbit hole of one person's mind that day. 

(One person at a time.) 

## The natural break point in the Drill Down Method is between Day 3 and Day 4. That's always where I take a break when I'm doing it with someone if it's getting too intense to go straight through. Having that extra time to think about "what just happened" is good. Ideas will marinate long after the sessions last. The sessions will conjure up memories (angels and demons), if the person asking the questions that day is probing deeply.

### So here's the best partner schedule I've found:

### Do Day 1, 2, 3 with one person.

### Then switch and do Day 1, 2, 3 with the other person.

### THEN do Day 4 and 5 (super intense days!) with the first person again.

### SWITCH and do Day 4 and 5.

### And then Day 6 and 7 same thing...

_It's intense, yes. But it's also hella fun._

### (If either of you finds your message at any point email me to test it out on me!)

Btw, one good way to warm up is to do The Meyers Briggs Together:

Have one person ask the questions and enter the data online, while the other person concentrates on giving the best answers. Perfect for breaking the ice.

### [**Meyers-Briggs Website**](https://www.16personalities.com/free-personality-test)

GOOD LUCK!

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/23846521/comments/15341791)

I didn't get this the first time I read it, so wound up doing it differently... trading days back n forth Days 1-3 (so, spread over a week) then taking a weekend off then continuing with days 4, 5 & 6 splitting 90minutes half way. twas a good thing that the person I partnered up with is someone who has a lot of parallels in terms of world view but through a COMPLETELY different life experience, ways of moving through the world, art form (she's an artist/animator/storyteller, I'm a musician/communitycatalyst/clown) ... so we were able to bounce off eachother in a really generative authentic helpful way. But yeah I can see how some of the amnesia would be nice, for the next time i do this with someone :)
